steps.title: Find the sale price using the discount
steps.content:
When the sale price is 80% less than the regular price, it equals 20% of the regular price (since 100% − 80% = 20%).
$$\text{Sale price} = 0.20 \times 11.70 = 2.34$$

steps.title: Use the cost-to-sale relationship and solve for cost
steps.content:
We're told the sale price is 30% greater than the store's cost. This means:
$$\text{Sale price} = \text{Cost} \times 1.30$$
Substituting the sale price we found:
$$2.34 = \text{Cost} \times 1.30$$
Dividing both sides by 1.30:
$$\text{Cost} = \frac{2.34}{1.30} = 1.80$$
The store's cost for the shirt is \$1.80.
